binaries.title: EhStorClass.sys (Enhanced Storage Class Driver for IEEE 1667) - Missing device-type check before installing silo-specific IRP completion routine

changed_functions.name: FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1C0005790)
key_changes: NEW device-type gate added at patched 0x1c0005930: reads DWORD [device_extension+0x130], masks with 0xf000, compares to 0x6000 via sbb-based comparison. When (field & 0xf000) >= 0x6000, the function skips IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext + completion-routine setup and instead uses IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation + IofCallDriver (pass-through). This is the sole behavioral delta.

Epilogue restructured: the STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER reject path (rejected IOCTL 0x4d004 silo commands) now stores the status into r14d and reaches a single early-return check ('test r14d, r14d; js <epilogue>' at 0x1c0005927). This is behaviorally equivalent to the unpatched direct jump to the epilogue after IofCompleteRequest — a refactoring artifact, not a new security check.

The forwarding method selection is now gated by the device type: in the unpatched version the dispatch flags (r8b/sil) directly control copy-vs-skip and forward method; in the patched version both decisions are ANDed with the new device-type gate ((field < 0x6000) ? dispatch_flag : 0), so device types >= 0x6000 always get pass-through forwarding with no completion routine.

Completion routine address changed from 0x1c0005b50 (unpatched) to 0x1c0005ba0 (patched) — relocation artifact (this function grew 0x50 bytes, shifting all later code); the completion routine is byte-identical between builds.
WPP trace-string reference shifted — data-section relocation artifact.
changed_functions.security_notes: This function is the IRP dispatch handler registered via WdfDeviceInitAssignWdmIrpPreprocessCallback (WdfFunctions+0x248) in DriverEvtDeviceAdd (0x1c0013010) for IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL (0x0e, at 0x1c0013621/0x1c001362f) and IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL (0x0f, at 0x1c0013681/0x1c001368f) on IEEE 1667 disk PDOs. For SCSI (0x0f) on a silo device (ext+0x08 != 0) and IOCTL 0x4d004/0x4d005 (IEEE 1667 silo command) requests, the UNPATCHED version copies the IRP stack location down (IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext) and installs the completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50), which performs IEEE 1667 silo-specific processing: (1) acquires a WDF lock at extension+0x1E8, (2) parses SCSI VPD/CDB response descriptors (types 0x40/0x41/0x42) with length-checked field reads, (3) binary-searches a silo LBA-range table at extension+0x1D0 (count at extension+0x1D8, 0x18-byte entries) guarded by the entry count, (4) on a range match MODIFIES the SCSI response data — injecting CHECK CONDITION (status | 0x70), sense code 0x0220, and SRB status |= 0x80. For device types where [extension+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000, this silo interception is not intended, so rewriting the SCSI response corrupts the I/O semantics of a device that should have passed through untouched. The PATCHED version adds the device-type gate that prevents the completion routine from being installed for these device types, causing the IRP to pass through transparently. The change is correctly directed (patched is strictly more restrictive); the reachable impact is SCSI response corruption (integrity/availability), not memory corruption — the table walk is count-guarded and the descriptor parsing is length-checked.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED (FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) @ 0x1c0005790) — KEY FORWARDING SECTION ===
; After dispatch flags r8b/sil are computed:
0x1c0005916: xor     r8b, r8b          ; r8_2 = 0 for some paths
0x1c0005919: cmp     r8b, 0x1          ; check dispatch flag
0x1c000591d: jne     0x1c0005960       ; if r8b != 1, advance stack loc
; (copy stack location block — sets up completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50))
0x1c000591f: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rdx]
0x1c0005929: movups  xmmword [rdx-0x48], xmm0
...
0x1c000595e: jmp     0x1c000596e
; (advance stack location block)
0x1c0005960: inc     byte [rbp+0x43]   ; CurrentLocation++
0x1c0005967: mov     qword [rbp+0xb8], rax ; update stack loc ptr
; Forward:
0x1c000596e: ...
0x1c000597f: cmp     sil, 0x1
0x1c0005983: jne     0x1c0005997       ; IofCallDriver path
; (NO device-type check — completion routine ALWAYS installed when r8b==1)

=== PATCHED (FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) @ 0x1c0005790) — NEW DEVICE-TYPE GATE ===
0x1c0005927: test    r14d, r14d        ; status check
0x1c000592a: js      0x1c00059f8       ; if error, return (never forward)
0x1c0005930: mov     eax, dword [r13+0x130]  ; *** NEW: read device ext field ***
0x1c0005937: mov     r8d, 0x6000       ; *** NEW: compare value ***
0x1c000593d: mov     rcx, qword [rsi+0xb8] ; current stack loc
0x1c0005944: and     eax, 0xf000       ; *** NEW: mask ***
0x1c0005949: cmp     eax, r8d          ; *** NEW: compare (field&0xf000) with 0x6000 ***
0x1c000594c: sbb     dl, dl            ; dl = (field < 0x6000) ? 0xFF : 0x00
0x1c000594e: and     dl, dil           ; dl &= dispatch_flag_rdi
0x1c0005951: cmp     eax, r8d
0x1c0005954: sbb     al, al            ; al = (field < 0x6000) ? 0xFF : 0x00
0x1c0005956: and     al, bpl           ; al &= dispatch_flag_rbp
0x1c0005959: cmp     al, r9b           ; compare with 1 (r9=1)
0x1c000595c: jne     0x1c000599f       ; if NOT 1: SKIP completion routine (advance)
; When field >= 0x6000: al=0 → jne taken → pass-through (NO completion routine)
; When field <  0x6000: al=dispatch_flag → completion routine only if flag set

security_findings.function: FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) (EhStorClass WDM IOCTL/SCSI IRP preprocess callback)
security_findings.entry_point: IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL (IOCTL 0x4d004/0x4d005 — IEEE 1667 silo command) and IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL (SCSI pass-through) to IEEE 1667 disk PDOs
call_chain: WDF framework dispatches to WDM preprocess callback FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) (registered in DriverEvtDeviceAdd (0x1c0013010) for MajorFunction 0x0e and 0x0f)

FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790): dispatches based on MajorFunction byte, sets up completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) via IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext
IofCallDriver / WdfIoTarget forward IRP to lower storage port driver

Completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) runs: acquires ext+0x1E8 lock, binary-searches ext+0x1D0 silo range table, parses/modifies SCSI VPD response, injects CHECK CONDITION sense data
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing device-type validation (CWE-697) before applying IEEE 1667 silo-specific processing to an incompatible device type (CWE-843) — the silo completion routine rewrites SCSI responses for devices that should be passed through untouched
security_findings.description: The function FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) is the WDM IRP preprocess callback for IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL and IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL on IEEE 1667 disk PDOs. For SCSI requests (MajorFunction 0x0f) on a silo device (ext+0x08 != 0) and IOCTL 0x4d004/0x4d005 silo command requests (where the SystemBuffer bytes at offset 0x24/0x25 do NOT match the magic values 0xa2/0xee or 0xb5/0xee that would trigger rejection), the UNPATCHED version calls IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext and installs the completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) with context = device object (arg1) and Control = 0xC0 (SL_INVOKE_ON_SUCCESS | SL_INVOKE_ON_ERROR). The completion routine then performs IEEE 1667 silo-specific post-processing: (1) it acquires a WDF lock at device_extension+0x1E8; (2) it parses SCSI VPD/CDB response descriptors (type codes 0x40/0x41/0x42) from a response buffer, with each field read bounds-checked against the descriptor length (e.g. cmp rax, rcx; ja <exit> at 0x1c0005c60 and 0x1c0005c88, length checks at 0x1c0005dfc and 0x1c0005e2f); (3) it binary-searches a silo LBA-range table at device_extension+0x1D0 (count at extension+0x1D8, each entry 0x18 bytes), a walk that is guarded by the entry count (the search runs only when count > 1, cmp r10d,1;jbe at 0x1c000610d, and a count of 0 skips every table dereference, cmp edx,r10d;jnb at 0x1c0006145); (4) on a range match it MODIFIES the SCSI response data — setting the status byte to CHECK CONDITION (| 0x70), writing sense code 0x0220, and setting SRB status flag |= 0x80. This response rewriting is only appropriate for IEEE 1667 silo device types that warrant silo interception. For device PDOs where device_extension+0x130 (a device type/capability field) has bits 12-15 >= 6 (i.e., [ext+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000), the unpatched driver still installs the completion routine and can therefore corrupt the SCSI sense/status of an otherwise-healthy response for a device that should have been passed through untouched. The PATCHED version adds a gate at 0x1c0005930 that checks [ext+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000; when true, it skips the completion routine entirely (uses IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation instead of IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext) and forwards via plain IofCallDriver, making the IRP pass through transparently without silo-specific interception.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Target device: An IEEE 1667 silo PDO (ext+0x08 != 0) handled by EhStorClass.sys whose device extension has [ext+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000. This is a device-type/capability value set during enumeration/initialization, not per-request attacker input; reaching the path requires such a device to be present. 2. SCSI path: Send an IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL request (e.g., via IOCTL_SCSI_PASS_THROUGH or IOCTL_SCSI_PASS_THROUGH_DIRECT) to the PDO. The SRB Function byte at SRB+0x02 and CDB/VPD response data determine which completion-routine sub-path executes. 3. IOCTL path: Send DeviceIoControl with IOCTL 0x4d004 (CTL_CODE(FILE_DEVICE_CONTROLLER=4, Function=0x401, METHOD_BUFFERED, FILE_READ_DATA|FILE_WRITE_DATA)) and an input buffer of >= 0x26 bytes where SystemBuffer[0x24] is NOT 0xa2 and NOT 0xb5 (or SystemBuffer[0x25] != 0xee) — this bypasses the rejection check and causes the IRP to be forwarded WITH the completion routine. 4. The completion routine runs after the lower driver completes the IRP and processes the response through the silo-specific code paths.
security_findings.primitive: The demonstrable, reachable impact is an integrity/availability problem localized to the affected device: on the unpatched build, a silo device whose capability field is >= 0x6000 can have its SCSI response rewritten by the completion routine (CHECK CONDITION injected via | 0x70, sense code 0x0220 written, SRB status |= 0x80), turning a valid response into a spurious error and disrupting that device's I/O or IEEE 1667 command handling. No memory-corruption or control-flow primitive is supported by these binaries: the silo range-table walk is count-guarded (the zero-initialized extension state, count 0 with NULL pointer, dereferences nothing) and the VPD/descriptor parsing is length-checked before each access. There is therefore no demonstrable NULL dereference from the natural uninitialized state, no out-of-bounds read/write, no pool corruption, no information leak, and no path to kernel code execution in these two builds.
security_findings.assembly:
=== FULL UNPATCHED ASSEMBLY: FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790) @ 0x1c0005790 (ehstorclass_unpatched.sys) ===
0x1c0005790: mov     rax, rsp
0x1c0005793: mov     qword [rax+0x8], rbx
0x1c0005797: mov     qword [rax+0x10], rbp
0x1c000579b: mov     qword [rax+0x18], rsi
0x1c000579f: mov     qword [rax+0x20], rdi
0x1c00057a3: push    r14
0x1c00057a5: sub     rsp, 0x20
0x1c00057a9: mov     rax, qword [rel 0x1c0010c70]
0x1c00057b0: mov     rbp, rdx
0x1c00057b3: mov     r8, qword [rel 0x1c000f018]
0x1c00057ba: mov     r14, rcx
0x1c00057bd: mov     rdx, rcx
0x1c00057c0: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0010a50]
0x1c00057c7: mov     rax, qword [rax+0x650]
0x1c00057ce: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012198]          ; CFG dispatch: get device extension
0x1c00057d4: mov     r8, qword [rel 0x1c0010c70]
0x1c00057db: mov     rdi, rax                          ; rdi = device extension
0x1c00057de: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0010a50]
0x1c00057e5: mov     rdx, qword [rdi]                  ; ext->field_00
0x1c00057e8: mov     rax, qword [r8+0x9e0]
0x1c00057ef: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012198]          ; CFG dispatch: acquire lock
0x1c00057f5: mov     ebx, dword [rdi+0x8]              ; ebx = ext->isSilo (flag)
0x1c00057f8: mov     esi, dword [rdi+0x10]             ; esi = ext->flag_10
0x1c00057fb: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0010c70]
0x1c0005802: mov     rdx, qword [rdi]
0x1c0005805: mov     rax, qword [rcx+0x9e8]
0x1c000580c: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0010a50]
0x1c0005813: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012198]          ; CFG dispatch: release lock
0x1c0005819: mov     rdx, qword [rbp+0xb8]             ; rdx = IRP->Tail.Overlay.CurrentStackLocation
0x1c0005820: test    ebx, ebx                          ; is this a silo device?
0x1c0005822: jne     0x1c000583e                       ; yes → dispatch by MajorFunction
0x1c0005824: cmp     byte [rdx], 0xe                   ; MajorFunction == IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL?
0x1c0005827: jne     0x1c0005836
0x1c0005829: cmp     dword [rdx+0x18], 0x2d1400        ; IOCTL_STORAGE_QUERY_PROPERTY?
0x1c0005830: je      0x1c0005913
0x1c0005836: xor     sil, sil                          ; rsi = 0
0x1c0005839: jmp     0x1c0005916
; --- SCSI (0x0f) and IOCTL dispatch for silo devices (ebx != 0) ---
0x1c000583e: mov     al, byte [rdx]                    ; al = MajorFunction
0x1c0005840: cmp     al, 0xf                           ; IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL (SCSI)?
0x1c0005842: jne     0x1c0005874
0x1c0005844: mov     rax, qword [rdx+0x8]              ; SRB pointer
0x1c0005848: cmp     byte [rax+0x2], 0x28              ; SRB Function == 0x28?
0x1c000584c: jne     0x1c0005854
0x1c000584e: mov     r9d, dword [rax+0x18]             ; read SRB field
0x1c0005852: jmp     0x1c0005858
0x1c0005854: mov     r9d, dword [rax+0xc]              ; read different SRB field
0x1c0005858: mov     r8b, 0x1                          ; r8_2 = 1 (COPY stack loc + completion)
0x1c000585b: test    r8b, sil
0x1c000585e: sete    cl
0x1c0005861: bt      r9d, 0x13
0x1c0005866: setae   al
0x1c0005869: test    al, cl
0x1c000586b: setne   sil                               ; compute forward method flag
0x1c000586f: jmp     0x1c0005919
0x1c0005874: cmp     al, 0xe                           ; IRP_MJ_DEVICE_CONTROL?
0x1c0005876: jne     0x1c0005913                       ; no → rsi=1, skip completion
0x1c000587c: mov     ecx, dword [rdx+0x18]             ; ecx = IoControlCode
0x1c000587f: lea     eax, [rcx-0x4d004]                ; IOCTL 0x4d004/0x4d005?
0x1c0005885: test    eax, 0xffffffef
0x1c000588a: jne     0x1c00058fa                       ; no → check 0x4d02c
0x1c000588c: mov     rax, qword [rbp+0x18]             ; rax = IRP->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer
0x1c0005890: mov     cl, byte [rax+0x24]               ; SystemBuffer[0x24] (silo cmd byte)
0x1c0005893: cmp     cl, 0xa2
0x1c0005896: je      0x1c000589d
0x1c0005898: cmp     cl, 0xb5
0x1c000589b: jne     0x1c00058ef
0x1c000589d: cmp     byte [rax+0x25], 0xee             ; SystemBuffer[0x25] == 0xee?
0x1c00058a1: jne     0x1c00058ef                       ; magic mismatch → forward with completion
; --- Magic bytes MATCH → reject IOCTL ---
0x1c00058a3: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c000f0c8]
0x1c00058aa: lea     rax, [rel 0x1c000f0c8]
0x1c00058b1: cmp     rcx, rax
0x1c00058b4: je      0x1c00058d1
0x1c00058b6: cmp     byte [rcx+0x29], 0x3
0x1c00058ba: jb      0x1c00058d1
0x1c00058bc: mov     rcx, qword [rcx+0x18]
0x1c00058c0: lea     r8, [rel 0x1c000d6f0]             ; 'SiloCmdE' log string
0x1c00058c7: mov     edx, 0xa
0x1c00058cc: call    0x1c00048dc                       ; EtwWrite log
0x1c00058d1: mov     ebx, 0xc000000d                   ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
0x1c00058d6: xor     edx, edx
0x1c00058d8: mov     rcx, rbp                          ; rcx = IRP
0x1c00058db: mov     dword [rbp+0x30], ebx             ; IRP->IoStatus.Status
0x1c00058de: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012048]           ; IofCompleteRequest
0x1c00058e5: nop
0x1c00058ea: jmp     0x1c00059b8                       ; → return
; --- Magic bytes DON'T match → forward WITH completion routine ---
0x1c00058ef: mov     r8b, 0x1                          ; r8_2 = 1
0x1c00058f2: not     sil
0x1c00058f5: and     sil, r8b
0x1c00058f8: jmp     0x1c0005919
; --- Check IOCTL 0x4d02c ---
0x1c00058fa: lea     eax, [rcx-0x4d02c]
0x1c0005900: test    eax, 0xfffffffb
0x1c0005905: jne     0x1c0005913                       ; not 0x4d02c → rsi=1
0x1c0005907: xor     r8b, r8b                          ; r8_2 = 0 (SKIP completion)
0x1c000590a: not     sil
0x1c000590d: and     sil, 0x1
0x1c0005911: jmp     0x1c0005919
; --- label_1c0005913: rsi=1 ---
0x1c0005913: mov     sil, 0x1
0x1c0005916: xor     r8b, r8b                          ; r8_2 = 0
0x1c0005919: cmp     r8b, 0x1
0x1c000591d: jne     0x1c0005960                       ; r8b != 1 → IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation
; *** IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext + install completion routine ***
0x1c000591f: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rdx]
0x1c0005922: lea     rcx, [rel 0x1c0005b50]            ; FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) = completion routine
0x1c0005929: movups  xmmword [rdx-0x48], xmm0
0x1c000592d: movups  xmm1, xmmword [rdx+0x10]
0x1c0005931: movups  xmmword [rdx-0x38], xmm1
0x1c0005935: movups  xmm0, xmmword [rdx+0x20]
0x1c0005939: movups  xmmword [rdx-0x28], xmm0
0x1c000593d: movsd   xmm1, qword [rdx+0x30]
0x1c0005942: movsd   qword [rdx-0x18], xmm1
0x1c0005947: mov     byte [rdx-0x45], 0x0              ; clear Control
0x1c000594b: mov     rax, qword [rbp+0xb8]            ; reload stack loc ptr
0x1c0005952: mov     qword [rax-0x10], rcx            ; set CompletionRoutine = FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50)
0x1c0005956: mov     qword [rax-0x8], r14              ; set Context = device object
0x1c000595a: mov     byte [rax-0x45], 0xc0            ; Control = SL_INVOKE_ON_SUCCESS|ERROR
0x1c000595e: jmp     0x1c000596e
; *** IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation ***
0x1c0005960: inc     byte [rbp+0x43]                   ; IRP->CurrentLocation++
0x1c0005963: lea     rax, [rdx+0x48]
0x1c0005967: mov     qword [rbp+0xb8], rax             ; update CurrentStackLocation
; *** Forward IRP — NO device-type check in unpatched ***
0x1c000596e: mov     rax, qword [rel 0x1c0010c70]
0x1c0005975: mov     rdx, r14                          ; device object
0x1c0005978: mov     rcx, qword [rel 0x1c0010a50]
0x1c000597f: cmp     sil, 0x1
0x1c0005983: jne     0x1c0005997                       ; sil != 1 → IofCallDriver
0x1c0005985: mov     rax, qword [rax+0x110]            ; WDF forward method
0x1c000598c: mov     r8, rbp                           ; r8 = IRP
0x1c000598f: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012198]           ; CFG dispatch
0x1c0005995: jmp     0x1c00059b6
0x1c0005997: mov     rax, qword [rax+0x100]            ; get target device
0x1c000599e: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012198]           ; CFG dispatch: get lower dev
0x1c00059a4: mov     rcx, rax                          ; target device
0x1c00059a7: mov     rdx, rbp                          ; IRP
0x1c00059aa: call    qword [rel 0x1c0012070]           ; IofCallDriver
0x1c00059b1: nop
0x1c00059b6: mov     ebx, eax                          ; save status
0x1c00059b8: mov     rbp, qword [rsp+0x38]
0x1c00059bd: mov     eax, ebx                          ; return value
0x1c00059bf: mov     rbx, qword [rsp+0x30]
0x1c00059c4: mov     rsi, qword [rsp+0x40]
0x1c00059c9: mov     rdi, qword [rsp+0x48]
0x1c00059ce: add     rsp, 0x20
0x1c00059d2: pop     r14
0x1c00059d4: retn

breakpoints.why: Entry to the dispatch function. At this point: rbp = IRP (arg2), r14 = device object (arg1), rdi = device extension pointer, ebx = ext->isSilo flag (+0x08), esi = ext->flag (+0x10). Inspect rdi to examine the device extension, especially [rdi+0x130] to determine if the device type triggers the patched gate (value & 0xf000 >= 0x6000). Also inspect [rdi+0x1D0] (silo range table pointer) and [rdi+0x1D8] (table count); the completion routine's table walk is guarded by the count (it dereferences the table only when count > 1, and count 0 skips it), so the zero-initialized state does not fault.

This is the IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext + completion-routine-install block in the UNPATCHED binary. If this breakpoint hits, the completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) will be installed. At this point, rdx = current IO_STACK_LOCATION, rbp = IRP. Check whether the device type at [rdi+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000 — if so, this code is executing for a device type that the patched build excludes (the patch redirects to pass-through).

The completion routine entry. When this fires for a device with [ext+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000, the silo interception is running on a device type the patched build excludes. arg2 (rdx) = IRP, arg3 (r8) = device object (context set at 0x1c0005956). Watch the count-guarded table walk at 0x1c0006125 (access to silo range table at ext+0x1D0) and the SRB response rewrite at 0x1c00061f7-0x1c0006233 (CHECK CONDITION / sense 0x0220 / SRB status |= 0x80).

The table walk accesses [ext+0x1D0 + index*0x18] where ext = device extension. This access is guarded by the entry count at ext+0x1D8: the binary-search loop is entered only when count > 1 (cmp r10d,1;jbe at 0x1c000610d) and a count of 0 skips the dereference entirely (cmp edx,r10d;jnb at 0x1c0006145). The zero-initialized extension state (count 0, pointer NULL) therefore does not fault here.

watch: ebx (at 0x1c0005820) = ext->isSilo flag — must be non-zero to enter the dispatch branches that set up the completion routine
rdi+0x130 = device type/capability DWORD — UNPATCHED code ignores this; PATCHED gates on ([rdi+0x130] & 0xf000) >= 0x6000
rdi+0x1D0 = silo range table pointer (used in completion routine) — dereferenced only under the count guard at ext+0x1D8
rdi+0x1D8 = silo range table entry count
rdi+0x1E8 = WDF lock handle — acquired/released in completion routine
[rbp+0x18] = IRP->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer — SystemBuffer[0x24]/[0x25] checked for magic 0xa2/0xee or 0xb5/0xee
key_offsets: 0x1c0005819: mov rdx, [rbp+0xb8] — loads CurrentStackLocation into rdx for all subsequent dispatch
0x1c0005820: test ebx, ebx — silo device check; jne at 0x1c0005822 enters MajorFunction dispatch
0x1c000588c: mov rax, [rbp+0x18] — loads SystemBuffer for magic byte check
0x1c0005890: mov cl, [rax+0x24] — reads SystemBuffer[0x24] (silo command byte)
0x1c000591f: movups [rdx-0x48] — IoCopyCurrentIrpStackLocationToNext START (completion routine installed here in UNPATCHED)
0x1c0005952: mov [rax-0x10], rcx — sets CompletionRoutine = FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) (installs the silo completion routine without a device-type check)
PATCHED 0x1c0005930: mov eax, [r13+0x130] — THE NEW CHECK that the unpatched binary lacks entirely
PATCHED 0x1c0005944: and eax, 0xf000 — mask device type field
PATCHED 0x1c0005949: cmp eax, r8d (0x6000) — device-type gate comparison
PATCHED 0x1c000595c: jne 0x1c000599f — skip completion routine when device type >= 0x6000
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Identify or create an IEEE 1667 PDO device handled by EhStorClass.sys (e.g., a BitLocker To Go USB drive, or a crafted USB storage device claiming IEEE 1667 compliance). The device must have its PDO extension field at offset 0x130 set to a value where (field & 0xf000) >= 0x6000. 2. Open a handle to the device PDO or its silo device interface (e.g., via SetupDiGetClassDevs with the IEEE 1667 device interface GUID, then CreateFile on the device path). 3a. IOCTL path: Call DeviceIoControl(hDevice, CTL_CODE(FILE_DEVICE_CONTROLLER=4, 0x401, METHOD_BUFFERED, FILE_READ_DATA|FILE_WRITE_DATA)=0x4d004, inBuf, sizeof(inBuf), outBuf, sizeof(outBuf), ...). The input buffer must be >= 0x26 bytes. Set inBuf[0x24] to any value EXCEPT 0xa2 or 0xb5 (or set inBuf[0x25] to any value except 0xee) to avoid the rejection check at 0x1c0005890-0x1c00058a1. The IRP will be forwarded with the completion routine installed. 3b. SCSI path: Use IOCTL_SCSI_PASS_THROUGH or IOCTL_SCSI_PASS_THROUGH_DIRECT to send a SCSI command (e.g., INQUIRY, READ CAPACITY, or MODE SENSE) to the device. This generates IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL which triggers the completion routine path. 4. After the lower driver completes the IRP, the completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) runs and processes the response through silo-specific code paths that are invalid for this device type.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On the UNPATCHED binary, for a silo device whose capability field (ext+0x130 & 0xf000) is >= 0x6000, the completion routine runs and, on a range match, rewrites the SCSI response: the status byte gets CHECK CONDITION (| 0x70), sense code 0x0220 is written, and SRB status is set (|= 0x80). The observable effect is a valid SCSI request returning a spurious error for that device. On the PATCHED binary, the completion routine is never installed for these device types (the jne at 0x1c000595c redirects to IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation + IofCallDriver), so the IRP passes through and the original lower-driver response is returned unmodified. These binaries do not demonstrate a memory-safety fault (NULL deref, OOB, pool corruption) on this path: the table walk is count-guarded and the descriptor parsing is length-checked.
debugging_aids.notes: The device extension field at offset 0x130 stores a device type/capability DWORD that is zeroed during initial allocation (memset 0x1F0 bytes in DriverEvtDeviceAdd (0x1c0013010)) and set later during device initialization. The patched check gates on bits 12-15 (nibble at 0xf000) being >= 6 (0x6000). This field likely represents an IEEE 1667 silo type or storage device capability that determines whether silo-specific IRP interception is appropriate. The IRP being processed has its IO_STACK_LOCATION at [rbp+0xb8]; note that 'Overlay_1' in the pseudocode = IRP->Tail.Overlay.CurrentStackLocation. The completion routine FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50) returns STATUS_SUCCESS (0) and propagates PendingReturned, so it always allows IRP completion to proceed — the damage occurs during the processing itself, not from preventing completion. Symbols: load EhStorClass.pdb (referenced in binary at 0x1c000dbf4). ASLR: the image base is 0x1c0000000 in this analysis; add the offset from the actual loaded base.

summary: The patch to EhStorClass.sys (Enhanced Storage Class Driver for IEEE 1667 devices) adds a single device-type check to the data I/O dispatch handler (FilterDeviceEvtWdmIoctlIrpPreprocess (0x1c0005790)). The unpatched version installs an IEEE 1667 silo-specific IRP completion routine (FilterDeviceWdmIoctlCompletionRoutine (0x1c0005b50)) for SCSI (IRP_MJ_INTERNAL_DEVICE_CONTROL) and IOCTL 0x4d004 silo-command requests on silo PDOs (ext+0x08 != 0), regardless of the device's type. The completion routine performs silo-specific post-processing — parsing SCSI VPD/CDB response descriptors, searching an LBA range table, and on a range match rewriting the SCSI response (CHECK CONDITION, sense 0x0220, SRB status |= 0x80). This interception is only appropriate for device types whose extension field at offset 0x130 has bits 12-15 < 6. The patched version adds a gate at 0x1c0005930 that checks [ext+0x130] & 0xf000 >= 0x6000 and, when true, skips the completion routine entirely (IoSkipCurrentIrpStackLocation + IofCallDriver pass-through). The change is correctly directed (patched is strictly more restrictive) and the completion routine is byte-identical between builds. The demonstrable, reachable impact is integrity/availability: for a silo device whose capability field is >= 0x6000, the unpatched driver can rewrite a valid SCSI response into a spurious error, disrupting that device's I/O. No memory-corruption or code-execution primitive is supported by these binaries — the range-table walk is count-guarded and the descriptor parsing is length-checked. Severity: Low.
